page: Microsoft Azure E16-8s_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the ESv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with a base frequency of 2.6 GHz. The processor features a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography. The server is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most standard compute and memory operations. The server achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 4696.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 8274.8. Memory bandwidth is measured at 414.65 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 40.84 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, performance bottlenecks appear in highly parallelized or intensive mathematical workloads. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and the PassMark CPU physics test fall into the bottom 25 percent of benchmarks. Furthermore, medium-sized LLM inference is a notable weakness, with prompt processing speed dropping to a poor 14.81 tokens per second.

Given the high memory density of 16.0 GB per vCPU, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. It is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, where it handles 6.84 million SET operations per second, and static web serving at 6.54 GB/sec. It is less suitable for heavy multi-threaded compute tasks or large-scale machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-8s_v4 is a memory-optimized server from the ESv4 family, featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz and 128.0 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks show average results for standard tasks, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 4696.0 and a Redis throughput of 6.84 million SET operations per second. However, it exhibits performance trade-offs in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-model LLM inference. This server is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as databases, caching layers, and static web serving, where high memory capacity is prioritized over dense multi-core compute performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8s_v4 is a memory-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8s_v4 is a memory-optimized server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and average database and caching performance.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, designed for caching, databases, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 35 MB L3 cache
128.0 GB of system memory providing a high 16.0 GB per vCPU ratio
No local storage included, but compatible with Premium Storage
Average performance in database operations and static web serving
Reduced efficiency in multi-threaded decompression and medium LLM inference
Best suited for databases, Redis caching, and memory-bound applications
